message:
feat(refresh-ledger): regression_verdict for CI bench hard-gates [coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.24]

Sixth ibuuh.24 slice this session — complementary surface to the
operator-tracing emit_tracing_summary (4d4b168d). Operator tracing
is a soft visibility signal in default-level logs; regression_verdict
is the hard-gate consumer for CI bench harnesses that need to
exit non-zero on real regressions.

What landed:
- pub struct RegressionVerdictThresholds {
      warning_duration_pct, failure_duration_pct
  }
  Caller-supplied thresholds so different bench harnesses can tune
  tolerance (a noisy benchmark runner picks looser values than a
  deterministic CI worker). defaults() returns 15% warning / 30%
  failure calibrated for cass workloads.
- Fallible try_new(warning, failure) constructor that REJECTS
  internally-inconsistent configurations (warning >= failure would
  never raise a warning before the failure trips). Returns
  Err(&'static str) with an explicit message so a project misordering
  thresholds gets a clean error rather than silent "always fails".
- pub enum RegressionVerdict { Clean, Warning{...}, Failure{...} }
  Tagged via #[serde(tag = "verdict", rename_all = "snake_case")] so
  CI runners persist the verdict JSON for PR comments + dashboards.
- pub fn should_fail_build(&self) -> bool — convenience for bench
  harnesses to write `if v.should_fail_build() { exit(1); }` without
  matching every variant.
- impl RefreshLedgerEvidenceComparison::regression_verdict(&thresholds)
  — pure function. Inclusive-threshold semantics:
    * duration_pct >= failure_duration_pct ⇒ Failure
    * duration_pct >= warning_duration_pct ⇒ Warning
    * else ⇒ Clean

Defensive degenerate-case handling:
- aggregate_duration_delta_pct == None (baseline missing or empty)
  ⇒ Clean (no signal to gate on; refusing to fail CI on missing
  baseline data is a deliberate policy choice).
- Negative duration delta (improvement) ⇒ always Clean regardless
  of threshold polarity. Pinning the sign explicitly defends against
  a future maintainer who passes a negative warning_duration_pct
  (e.g. to gate on improvements as a positive signal) and would
  otherwise see every steady-state run trip.

Three golden gates:
- regression_verdict_categorizes_each_band_and_handles_degenerate_cases
  Pin tier semantics + boundary cases (15.0 inclusive ⇒ Warning,
  30.0 inclusive ⇒ Failure) + improvement-cleanness +
  None-data-cleanness. should_fail_build() pin on each variant.
- regression_verdict_thresholds_try_new_rejects_inconsistent_configurations
  Reject warning > failure, warning == failure, NaN, Infinity.
  Error message must contain "strictly less" so an operator
  reading the message understands the constraint.
- regression_verdict_serializes_with_snake_case_verdict_tag
  Pin the verdict tag + nested duration_delta_pct + threshold_pct
  fields. CI runners parse this JSON; a regression in the tag
  shape would silently break every consumer.

29/29 lib indexer::refresh_ledger tests green via
rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/rch_target_cass_p2 cargo test
--lib indexer::refresh_ledger.

Six ibuuh.24 slices this session (complete benchmark + observability
pipeline):
- 1ffd8494 RefreshLedgerEvidence single-run vocabulary
- 95961840 .lexical-refresh-evidence.json sidecar artifact
- e2e002ba RefreshLedgerEvidenceComparison cross-run comparator
- 4d4b168d emit_tracing_summary severity-tiered helper
- 64c8db93 publish-path wiring (auto cross-run tracing per publish)
- this commit RegressionVerdict for CI bench hard-gates

Operators get auto-tracing on every publish; bench-CI runners get
a structured verdict to gate the build.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
